data.description:
# Idea: self-hosted Renovate on Gerrit ŌĆö peer-pattern dependency updates through the two gates

Captured 2026-07-09 during the OSS v1.0.0 front-door research (epic breaded-ammonitic-elephant). The APPROVED v1.0 design is Dependabot advisory PRs on the GitHub mirror (github-actions ecosystem only, monthly; PRs are unmergeable notifications the maintainer lands via Gerrit; lockdown bot exempts dependabot[bot]) ŌĆö see story cardiac-lush-chihuahua. This idea records the researched alternative that would eliminate that departure from the Gerrit-peer pattern, for possible post-1.0 adoption.

## The finding

Renovate (Mend.io ŌĆö the leading cross-platform dependency bot; what ruff/uv use) has a native self-hosted Gerrit platform (`platform=gerrit`, all Gerrit 3.x): it pushes each dependency bump directly as a **Gerrit Change** ŌĆö no GitHub PRs ŌĆö so updates flow through the same `LLM-Review` + `Verified` gates as human changes. That is the Wikimedia-LibraryUpgrader / OpenStack-requirements-bot pattern, off the shelf, and it matches rebar's dogfooding ethos (the dependency bot is just another contributor under the two-vote merge policy).

- Docs: https://docs.renovatebot.com/modules/platform/gerrit/ and https://docs.renovatebot.com/modules/platform/
- Mechanics: Gerrit bot account + HTTP credentials; SSH cloning supported (discussion #39625); metadata via a `Renovate-Branch` commit footer (needs Gerrit Ōēź3.6, satisfied); `autoApprove` exists but would stay OFF (our gates vote); `statusCheckNames` can map Renovate checks (e.g. minimumReleaseAge) onto Gerrit labels.

## Why it was NOT chosen for v1.0 (the blockers to re-evaluate)

1. **Experimental platform status**: Renovate's Gerrit support is officially experimental ŌĆö maintainers deprioritize Gerrit issues and allow breaking changes in minor releases. Re-evaluate when it is declared stable.
2. **Ops cost**: Renovate is a CLI needing a scheduled runner (cron GitHub Action or the Gerrit host) plus credentials ŌĆö a new moving part vs zero-ops Dependabot; disproportionate for a monthly, github-actions-only update stream.
3. **`rebar-ticket:` trailer integration**: the Verified gate requires a resolving rebar-ticket trailer on every commit; Renovate has a known open issue with commit trailers via `commitBody` on Gerrit (https://github.com/renovatebot/renovate/discussions/33306). Adoption needs one of: a working trailer config, a standing "dependency updates" ticket baked into the commit template, or a narrow bot exemption in the require-ticket check.
4. No Dependency Dashboard on Gerrit (built on Issues) ŌĆö cosmetic at this scale.

## Promotion sketch (if/when picked up)

- Trial on a scratch Gerrit project first: bot account, `platform=gerrit`, github-actions manager only; verify trailer handling end-to-end through both gates; verify the LLM-Review bot behaves sanely on bot-authored changes.
- If adopted: retire the Dependabot advisory-PR flow (delete .github/dependabot.yml or reduce to security-alerts posture), remove the lockdown-bot dependabot exemption, and update the decision records in docs/releasing.md + the dependabot.yml rationale that story cardiac-lush-chihuahua created.
- Revisit triggers: Renovate Gerrit platform declared stable; dependency-update volume growing beyond monthly-actions-only (e.g. a committed uv.lock); the advisory-PR flow proving annoying in practice.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] (To be designed at promotion ŌĆö this is a parked idea, not dispatchable work.)
data.title: Self-hosted Renovate on Gerrit: peer-pattern dependency updates through the two gates (post-1.0)
